newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The ~-9% move reflects a continued chart pullback / broader de-rating, not a fresh event. FY26 guidance (revenue EUR460-500M, EBIT EUR53-58M) was reaffirmed in May and Q1 (order intake +90%, book-to-bill 1.7x, record backlog; seasonal Q1 EBIT loss) was reported pre-window.

sources.label: secunet Q1 2026 - order intake nearly doubles

verdicts.rankRationale: Scaling/sovereign-cybersecurity lens unchanged: no company-specific news June 9-23. Guidance reaffirmed in May; Q1's record order backlog (book-to-bill 1.7x) is constructive but pre-window. The -9% is a chart/de-rating move, not fundamentals. Hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Sovereign/regulated cyber demand; record backlog converting through 2026
verdicts.primaryRisk: Lumpy government order timing; rich multiple after prior run leaves room to de-rate
verdicts.bull: Order intake +90% and 1.7x book-to-bill underpin a reaffirmed EUR460-500M FY26 guide; sovereign-cyber tailwind.
verdicts.neutral: Seasonal Q1 EBIT loss and price pullback offset a strong backlog; story intact but no new catalyst.
verdicts.bear: Government order timing is lumpy; stock down ~23% over 90 days signals fading momentum.
